original_abstract: Developing alkali metal-based metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) with permanent porosity remains a significant challenge due to the high coordination affinity of alkali ions for solvating molecules like water. Despite this difficulty, such materials offer distinct gravimetric advantages for gas adsorption applications due to their lightweight nature. Herein, we report the synthesis of a novel sodium-based microporous MOF, HIAM-111, assembled from an octacarboxylate linker and 8-connected Naâ‚„ clusters. The resulting structure features a unique 3Â²,4Â²,8-c topology and exhibits exceptional architectural stability. Notably, HIAM-111 possesses a BET surface area of 1561 mÂ²/g, a value that significantly exceeds those of previously reported Na-MOFs. Adsorption studies reveal that HIAM-111 is highly effective for the separation of Câ‚‚Hâ‚‚/COâ‚‚ mixtures and the single-step purification of Câ‚‚Hâ‚„ from ternary Câ‚‚Hâ‚„/Câ‚‚Hâ‚‚/Câ‚‚Hâ‚† streams, maintaining high adsorption capacities throughout. This work provides a template for the rational design of robust, highly porous alkali-metal frameworks for advanced gas separation technologies.
humanized_abstract: Although numerous attempts have been made, MOFs based on alkali metals with permanent porosity are rarely reported owing to the strong coordination interaction of alkali metals with solvent molecules, such as water. Nevertheless, MOFs based on alkali metals often possess a gravimetric benefit for gas adsorption due to their low density. Herein, a sodium-based microporous MOF, namely HIAM-111, composed of an octacarboxylate ligand and 8-connected Naâ‚„ clusters is prepared. It possesses a 3Â²,4Â²,8-c unprecedented 3D network topology with excellent structural stability, which affords a BET surface area of 1561 mÂ²/g, a value far larger than that of all reported Na-MOFs. Additional experiments show that HIAM-111 is favorable for separation of the Câ‚‚Hâ‚‚/COâ‚‚ mixture and can efficiently purify the ternary mixture of Câ‚‚Hâ‚„/Câ‚‚Hâ‚‚/Câ‚‚Hâ‚† by a single adsorption step with excellent adsorption capacities at all times in the adsorption process. Our work offers an important route for the design of alkali-metal-based stable and highly porous MOFs and the corresponding potential application in gas separation.
